title: T-094 — SLO session-cookie clearing + Supabase session revoke (FT-034)
prompt:
# T-094 — SLO session-cookie clearing + Supabase session revoke (FT-034)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-034** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (S effort)

## Goal
SLO clears cookies + revokes Supabase session.

## Implementation contract
1. slo.ts adds `revokeLocalSession(userId, cookies)`: clears sb-access/sb-refresh cookies, calls supabase.auth.admin.signOut.
2. SLO route invokes the helper after assertion verification.
3. Test asserts cookies are cleared (Set-Cookie with Max-Age=0) and signOut is called once.

## Files (scope)
- packages/auth/src/saml/slo.ts [modify]
- packages/auth/src/saml/__tests__/slo.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/api/auth/saml/logout/route.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a successful SLO, when the handler completes, then sb-access and sb-refresh cookies are set with Max-Age=0.
2. Given a successful SLO, when the handler completes, then supabase.auth.admin.signOut has been called for the user.
3. Given a failed SLO (bad assertion), when the handler runs, then no cookie clearing happens.

## Test strategy
- RED: write slo.test.ts asserting cookie clearing + signOut call before implementation.
- Mock supabase admin via vi.fn().
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/auth test slo`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not clear cookies on failed SLO (info-leak vector).
- Do not call signOut without user_id resolution.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
